name: Niquests (HTTP/3-capable Requests drop-in replacement)

auth.notes: HTTP client library; auth via request params

performance.notes: HTTP/3 and HTTP/2 multiplexing enables higher throughput than requests

agent_readiness.notes: Drop-in replacement for requests with HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 support. import niquests as requests — full API compatibility. import niquests; r = niquests.get('https://example.com'). Session with multiplexing: s = niquests.Session(); s.get(url1); s.get(url2) — multiplexes over single connection. HTTP/3 via qh3. Async: import niquests; async with niquests.AsyncSession() as s: r = await s.get(url). Concurrent requests without threading: s.gather(). Waterfall avoidance. Type annotations throughout. Response.json(), .text, .content. Retry via urllib3. Compatible with requests-toolbelt. Replaces requests with minimal code change.

gotchas: Newer library — less battle-tested than requests at massive scale
Some requests extensions (custom auth adapters) may need updates
AsyncSession API differs slightly from httpx async patterns
best_when: Drop-in requests replacement needing HTTP/2 or HTTP/3
High-throughput API clients benefiting from connection multiplexing
When migrating from requests with minimal code change
avoid_when: When httpx is already in use (similar capabilities, larger community)
When HTTP/2+ is not needed and requests works
summary: Niquests is a drop-in requests replacement with HTTP/2 and HTTP/3. import niquests as requests — API compatible. Session() multiplexes connections. AsyncSession() for async. s.gather() for concurrent. HTTP/3 via qh3. Type annotations. Apache-2.0. Best for requests codebases needing HTTP/2+ without major refactor. Less community than httpx. qh3 for HTTP/3 support.
